\"Mr. Jaime Buelta\" Packt book OR cours:
Jaime Buelta is a software architect and author who has written several books for Packt Publishing. His work primarily focuses on Python programming, automation, and software architecture[1][2][3].

### Books by Jaime Buelta (Packt)
*   ***Python Automation Cookbook***: This book provides practical recipes for automating business processes, such as report generation, email marketing, and code testing[1][4]. A second edition of this book is also available[2][5].
*   ***Python Architecture Patterns***: This book covers advanced topics including API design, event-driven structures, and package management in Python[6].
*   ***Hands-On Docker for Microservices with Python***: This book guides readers on structuring large systems, encapsulating them using Docker, and deploying them with Kubernetes[2][7].

You can find these titles and additional technical resources by searching for "Jaime Buelta" directly on the **[Packt website](https://www.packt.com)**[8][2]. Packt offers a subscription-based library service ("Packt+") that provides access to their collection of books and video courses[9][10].

\"Mr. Jaime Buelta\" linkedin github:
Jaime Buelta is a professional programmer and Python developer known for his work in fields such as aerospace, networking, and finance[1][2].

*   **GitHub:** You can find his GitHub profile at [github.com/jaimebuelta](https://github.com/jaimebuelta)[3]. His profile features various projects, including `ffind` (a replacement for find), `django-docker-template`, and other utilities[4]. He is also the author of books such as the *Python Automation Cookbook*[1][5] and *Network Automation Cookbook*[6].
*   **LinkedIn:** While a direct link to his specific profile page was not returned in the search, you can typically locate him by searching "Jaime Buelta" on LinkedIn, where he is often identified by his professional background as a software developer and author[1].

\"Fuheng Wu\" Packt book OR course:
Fuheng Wu is the author of the book **"Distributed AI Systems: A practical guide to building scalable training, inference, and serving systems for production AI,"** published by **Packt Publishing** in June 2026[1][2][3].

There is currently no separate video course by Fuheng Wu listed; however, the book itself is a comprehensive technical guide designed to provide a "full path" from distributed training to production deployment[1][4][5]. It includes hands-on exercises at the end of every chapter and covers topics such as:

*   **GPU hardware and interconnects**[1][6]
*   **Parallelism strategies** (e.g., data parallelism, parameter sharding)[1][5][7]
*   **Distributed training techniques**[1][6]
*   **High-performance inference and production serving stacks**[1][6]
*   **Benchmarking and observability**[6][8]

The book is available through Packt's library (often accessible via subscription services like O'Reilly) and various major book retailers[2][9][10].

\"Fuheng Wu\" linkedin github:
Fuheng Wu (often referred to as **Henry Fuheng Wu**) is a software engineer known for his contributions to open-source projects and technology infrastructure[1][2].

### GitHub
You can find his projects on GitHub under the username **[henrywoo](https://github.com/henrywoo)**[3][1]. Notable contributions and repositories include:
*   **[athenadriver](https://github.com/uber/athenadriver)**: An open-source Amazon Athena database driver for Go, originally developed and maintained by him while at Uber[1][2][4].
*   **[kazam](https://github.com/henrywoo/kazam)**: A screencasting program for Linux[5].
*   **[distutils-extra](https://github.com/henrywoo/distutils-extra)**: Tools for enhancing Python's `distutils` or `setuptools`[6].
*   **[mae](https://github.com/henrywoo/mae)**: A repository related to Masked Autoencoders[7].

### Professional & Other Background
*   **Software Engineering:** He has worked as a software engineer, including at Uber (specifically on the ATG Infrastructure team)[2].
*   **Author:** He is the author of the book *Distributed AI Systems*[8] and the literary memoir *Above The Clouds* (published under the pen name Xuan Xin)[9][10].
*   **Patents:** He is listed as an inventor on patents related to datacenter detection and authentication techniques[11].

While his professional profile and project details are frequently cited in technical and open-source contexts[1][2][12], you can typically find his professional history and current networking via a search for "Henry Fuheng Wu" on LinkedIn.

\"Ken Huang\" Packt book OR course:
Ken Huang is a prolific author who has published several technical books with **Packt Publishing**, primarily focusing on AI, LLMs, and security[1][2][3].

### Books by Ken Huang (Packt)
*   **LLM Design Patterns: A Practical Guide to Building Robust and Efficient AI Systems** (Published May 2025)[4][5][6]
*   **AI-Native LLM Security: Threats, defenses, and best practices for building safe and trustworthy AI** (Published December 2025)[2]
*   **OpenClaw AI in Production: Architecture, design patterns, and engineering practices for AI agent platforms** (Expected June 2026)[7][8]

### Regarding Courses
While Ken Huang is a renowned expert who teaches graduate-level courses on Generative AI for Data Security at the **University of San Francisco**[4][9] and provides training through his firm, **DistributedApps.ai**[4][10], there is no specific standalone "Packt course" authored by him. However, his books are available through the **Packt+ library**, which provides access to his written content, and Packt often includes supplemental video materials or digital content alongside their book releases[1][11].

\"Sampriti Mitra\" Packt book OR course:
Sampriti Mitra is the author of the book **"System Design for the LLM Era: Patterns and principles for production-grade AI architecture,"** published by Packt[1][2][3].

The book focuses on the architectural blueprints and design principles required to build, scale, and maintain AI-powered applications that utilize large language models[4][3]. It covers various real-world scenarios, including AI-native IDEs, adaptive learning platforms, and intelligent search solutions[3][5][6]. 

While the content is available through the Packt library and subscription service[4][7], it is primarily structured as a book rather than a video-based course[3][8].

\"Sampriti Mitra\" linkedin github:
Sampriti Mitra is a software developer and tech writer with a focus on cloud technologies, system design, and AI[1][2][3].

### GitHub Profile
You can find her GitHub profile at **[github.com/Sampriti-Mitra](https://github.com/Sampriti-Mitra)**[1][2]. 

Her repositories and contributions include:
*   **Dialogflow Slack SDK:** A tool for integrating Dialogflow CX with Slack[1][4][5].
*   **Tech Interests:** Her projects and public contributions demonstrate experience with Go, AWS, GCP, Docker, Kubernetes, and Web3 technologies[1].
*   **Documentation & Contributions:** Her work has been recognized in official Google Cloud documentation for Dialogflow CX[5].

### Professional Presence
*   **Medium:** She writes technical articles on Medium, covering topics such as software engineering, cloud architecture, and AI[2].
*   **Substack:** She publishes a newsletter titled *Architecturally Speaking*, where she explores topics like AI-native IDEs and system design[3].
*   **LinkedIn:** While specific direct links to her personal LinkedIn profile may vary in search results, she is active in professional tech communities and often shares her articles and project updates on platforms like LinkedIn and Twitter[6][2].

\"Mr. Dipankar Sarkar\" Packt book OR co:
Dipankar Sarkar has authored or contributed to the following books published by Packt Publishing:

*   ***AI for Everyday Automation: 7 AI Workflows to Save Hours at Work Every Week*** (2026) – This is a recent book by Dipankar Sarkar published by Packt[1][2]. It focuses on practical AI workflows to improve workplace productivity[1].
*   ***Nginx 1 Web Server Implementation Cookbook*** (2011) – An earlier work by Dipankar Sarkar published by Packt, providing recipes for mastering the Nginx HTTP server[3][4].

While there are many resources available in the Packt library, these are the primary books authored by him under the Packt label[5][4]. You can typically find these titles and related content through the Packt Publishing website or their subscription platform[6][5].
